entries.text: So, I'm leaving Korea after being here
for almost 3 months and I thought I
would give you a room tour of my
apartment. It's not on Airbnb, so I
thought I would share because I don't
really see other people talking about
this place where I found my apartment
and it's cheaper than Airbnb and there's
no deposit or anything, so you know, and
I really like the place so I thought I
would share. Oh, by the way, this is not
sponsored. I wish somebody would pay me
for this. I'm giving you this out for
free. Let me give you a tour real quick.
So, let's start from the
beginning. That's my door. Door over
here. Right when you enter is a
mirror. There's a bunch of storage I
never used. I only use the actual closet
and that I don't have a lot of clothes.
I have
one suitcase with
me. So, next to the
door, we enter the bathroom.
again pretty standard bathroom right
there. There shower I did buy a shower
filter. Okay, I don't think it worked
but standard bathroom things. Moving on
from the
bathroom. Over here we
have the living space. My bed. I don't
know if you guys saw my London video,
but I had the same bed sheets in London.
I bought them myself at IKEA cuz I like
the design. Apparently, we have the same
taste with my host from Korea. Anyway,
this is the best part of the apartment.
It has two huge
windows, which means I get natural
lighting all day pretty much, and I love
it. This is my view from the window
number one. It's just a
street, but it's really cute. And then
the second one, I thought I'm not going
to get a lot of sunlight from here cuz
there's a bunch of buildings, but it's
really good. I get sunlight every day.
Also, one time I saw a cat over
[Music]
[Applause]
[Music]
there. Made my day for real. Look at
this cool looking lamp. And it works,
too.
How
cool, cool, cool. Anyway, that's my desk
where I spend a lot of time and edit
this video that you're watching right
now. Over here, we have the kitchen area
and the washing machine. The dryer is
actually over there. This has like a
kind of semi- dryer inside too, but I
still have to hang it there for like a
day. I have an almost full size fridge
cuz it's like I don't know if fridges in
Korea are just smaller cuz they're
shorter than me. But it's an almost full
size fridge, right? A huge thing cuz a
lot of apartments have like a mini
fridge. You can't put a lot. And I have
a freezer too, which is useful cuz I
freeze my food waste. Over here we have
storage for the kitchen. I keep my trash
in there and just stuff. I did have to
buy this utensils separately. But yeah,
kitchen. The buying utensils separately.
I did not have to do that in my other
places I stayed here cuz I stayed in
three different apartments with this
service. So, my two previous ones were
already equipped with everything. I just
didn't check the descriptions of the
third one, I guess, because it did not
have like the kitchen utensils and also
the towels in the bathroom. I had to buy
that too. It wasn't expensive cuz I went
to Daiso. But yeah, check the
description before you book things. So
the service that I used to book this
place is called Enor. It's a Korean
website for foreigners. So it's in
English. Everything is really
understandable cuz you know when you're
trying to book in other countries, you
don't speak the language. It's hard. As
I said, no deposit. So that's cool. You
do like sign a contract online when you
book places and so it's like legit and
everything. I think it's like different
homeowners. It's pretty much like an
Airbnb, but it's cheaper. Actually, the
way I discovered this place was on
Airbnb. I was looking for places to stay
in Korea and I found a place. I liked
it. I wanted to book it, but on in the
description, it said if you want to stay
long-term, use our website instead of
Airbnb. I went to their website and it
was $400 cheaper than Airbnb. The same
place that I wanted to book was $400
cheaper per month than on Airbnb. So, I
was like, I guess I'm renting through
their website. But I was cautious when I
was doing this cuz again, I've never
heard anyone talk about this service.
And when I was here 2 years ago using it
for the first time, they actually didn't
have a lot of like listings on the
website. They had like 30 places. But
since the one I saw on Airbnb and I
wanted was there and it already had like
reviews on Airbnb, I was like, "Okay,
this is a good place. I can rent it
through this website." But I was
cautious, but never had any problems.
So, I don't know. I had a good
experience and as I said, cheaper than
Airbnb cuz obviously Airbnb takes like
fees and stuff. The only thing when I
was booking 2 years ago, it said the
minimum stay you can book here is 1
month, like 30 days. But this time it
says 2 weeks is the minimum. So, they
lowered the minimum, which I think is
good. But also, I've never stayed in
Korea for less than 2 weeks. So, I don't
know who's coming to Korea for less than
two. Like, there's too much to do here.
And also when you're booking it there
there's like one thing that's kind of I
was like what every time you book a
place they ask you do you need bedding
like do you need the pillows and the
duvet and pillowcases and cover every
time I book a place they ask me that and
you have to pay like 50,0001 fee if you
want them to provide that. I was like
that's so random like why wouldn't they
just provide but I guess so people can
buy that themselves if they want. I
don't know. But yeah, and also the one
advice I have is cuz I never had a bad
experience with the places I stayed
through this. But one of the places I
stayed, actually the one that I filmed a
room tour on YouTube for, if you watch
that, that was booked through this
service to, that place was in a location
that didn't have any subway station near
the place. So, I did not really like
that. I couldn't just go to the subway
and leave to wherever I want to go. But
you can obviously check that they have
the locations before you book. You can
check that for yourself if you're okay
with the location. So definitely look at
the location. But that's when you book
anywhere probably. So yeah, right now
I'm staying in Mapogu. I've stayed here
before even on when I used Airbnb when I
was coming here like 5 years ago. I
liked already this area and I like that
it's like I can walk through the Hyundai
area and to Yamong if I want. I don't
have to take the subway. And also just
the area itself, it has a bunch of food
options, a bunch of restaurants and
whatnot. So I'm staying in Mapar right
now. The two previous apartments I
stayed at were inn known locations, but
that's because I wanted something
cheaper cuz obviously if you pick a
location that's better, it's more
expensive, even though it's less
expensive than Airbnb. So I was like,
I'm just going to stay affordable. And
one place didn't have access to subway
station right away. So that was not my
fave location. But the other location
wasn't like a trendy location, but it
was right next to a subway station, so I
could go anywhere from there. So, it was
cheaper to stay there. And even though
it didn't have all the cafes and trendy
places, I still like that. So, when
you're booking, just look for a subway
station with good like commutes. if
you're on a budget. But yeah, this time
I chose a like more expensive for their
website location, but I just wanted
something with a lot of local
restaurants and cafes, so I don't have
to use the subway every time. It has a
subway that's on on the green line, like
the best line in so it has both, but it
also has restaurants and cafes right
here, so I don't have to go through the
subway if I don't want to. But yeah,
that's the place. Hope this helps
someone because this really helped me
when I like discovered it really
randomly and I've never heard anyone
talk about this. I don't know why. Oh,
and also not only do they provide you
like this service provides housing every
like week I get an email from this
encore stay and they're like we're
having this like get together if you
want to meet us here. They hold like
meetings with the people that are
staying with their places and they're
inviting you. I never went because I'm
an introvert. I people scare me so I
never went. But yeah, as I said, not
sponsored, but I like the service. If
they want to pay me, please do. But
yeah, hope this helped someone cuz
again, every year you try to come to
Korea, it's more expensive. I wanted to
share that with everyone looking to stay
in. So, thank you for watching. K.
